blocks.content: Eli lasted forty-five minutes.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: He's actually asleep.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: He does this at every movie.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: He does this at every everything.
Eli was lying on his half of the blanket with his arms folded across his chest.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: He specifically suggested the movie.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: He suggested the going. He never said anything about the watching.
Savannah's phone buzzed. She looked at it, then at the screen, then back at it.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: My mom wants me home by ten.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: It's barely nine.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: I know. But Marcus is coming over.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Oh.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: Don't wake him up.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: We won't.
Savannah gathered her bag quietly, gave Daisy a look that communicated something, and then she was gone, winding through the people on blankets and lawn chairs toward the park path.
Eli breathed in the slow rhythm of someone with a genuinely clear conscience.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Should we wake him up?

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: And do what?

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: He lives on the other side of the park.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: He'll figure it out.
So they left him there with the blanket and the movie, two hundred people around them in the summer dark, and it got quieter somehow.
